[mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="84"] (דין כהן סומא וראיית בית הסתרים).
[mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="89"] (הנגע בעור בשרו, רק בחיצוניותו).
[mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="91"] (נגעי בתים בשורה היא להם כו').
[mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="96"] (באים על חטא). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="97"] (על עון לה"ר).
ע"ע [mafteach\_goterm file="ls32" goterm="מצורע"]. [mafteach\_goterm file="ls32" goterm="עבר הירדן"].

**English:**

## Nega'im

[mafteach_gopage file="ls32" gopage="78"] (Regarding the laws of *maros* [colors of nega'im] that combine with one another).
[mafteach_gopage file="ls32" gopage="79"] (Whether the signs of *tumah* are a *gezeiras hakasuv* [a scriptural decree]).
[mafteach_gopage file="ls32" gopage="84"] (The law of a blind *kohen* and the inspection of hidden areas).
[mafteach_gopage file="ls32" gopage="89"] (The *nega* on the skin of his flesh, only on its exterior).
[mafteach_gopage file="ls32" gopage="91"] (The *nega'im* of houses, that they are [judged] in a row, etc.).
[mafteach_gopage file="ls32" gopage="96"] (They come because of sin). [mafteach_gopage file="ls32" gopage="97"] (Regarding the sin of *lashon hara*).
See also [mafteach_goterm file="ls32" goterm="מצורע"] [mafteach_goterm file="ls32" goterm="עבר הירדן"].

_Section: נדה_

[mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="236"] (זמן ביהשמ"ש בנוגע לבדיקה).
[mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="38"]-[mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="237"] (ע"ד טיפול לשינוי זמן הוסת). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="240"] (טהרה ע"י רחיצה בנהרות וימים גם בלי כונה. גדר טומאה או איסור). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="244"] (המקור לדברי הרמב"ם דבן הנדה פגום).
ע"ע [mafteach\_goterm file="ls32" goterm="טהרת המשפחה"]. [mafteach\_goterm file="ls32" goterm="מקוה"]. [mafteach\_goterm file="ls32" goterm="נשואין"].

**English:**

## Niddah

[ftnref_ls32_236] (The timing of bein hashmashos regarding the bedikah).
[ftnref_ls32_38]-[ftnref_ls32_237] (Regarding treatment to alter the time of the veses). [ftnref_ls32_240] (Taharah through bathing in rivers and seas, even without kavanah. The definition of tumah or issur). [ftnref_ls32_244] (The source for the Rambam’s statement that a ben haniddah is pagum).
See also [ftnref_ls32_taharat_hamishpacha] (Taharas Hamishpacha). [ftnref_ls32_mikveh] (Mikveh). [ftnref_ls32_nisuin] (Nisuin).

## ניסן

_Section: ניסן_

**Hebrew:**

[mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="19"] (במנהג קריאת פ' הנשיא ונוסח היהי רצון. במנהג אי אמירת תחנון). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="53"] (בניסן עתידין להגאל). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="74"] (חודש הגאולה. נסי נסים). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="75"] (אין אומרים תחנון — וסליחת העונות נפעלת ממילא). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="184"] (בניסן עתי­דין להגאל). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="185"] (חודש הגאולה. כל יום מהחודש הוא בבחי' ר"ח). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="186"] (נסי נסים). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="192"] (בניסן עתידין להגאל). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="195"] (נסי נסים). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="204"] (חודש הגאולה). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="207"] (כנ"ל). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="216"] (נסי נסים).

**English:**

## Nissan

[mafteach_gopage file="ls32" gopage="19"] (Regarding the custom of reading the Parsha of the Nesi'im and the text of the Yehi Ratzon; regarding the custom of not saying Tachanun). [mafteach_gopage file="ls32" gopage="53"] (In Nissan they are destined to be redeemed). [mafteach_gopage file="ls32" gopage="74"] (The month of redemption; miracles of miracles). [mafteach_gopage file="ls32" gopage="75"] (Tachanun is not recited — and the forgiveness of sins is achieved automatically). [mafteach_gopage file="ls32" gopage="184"] (In Nissan they are destined to be redeemed). [mafteach_gopage file="ls32" gopage="185"] (The month of redemption; every day of the month is in the category of Rosh Chodesh). [mafteach_gopage file="ls32" gopage="186"] (Miracles of miracles). [mafteach_gopage file="ls32" gopage="192"] (In Nissan they are destined to be redeemed). [mafteach_gopage file="ls32" gopage="195"] (Miracles of miracles). [mafteach_gopage file="ls32" gopage="204"] (The month of redemption). [mafteach_gopage file="ls32" gopage="207"] (As above). [mafteach_gopage file="ls32" gopage="216"] (Miracles of miracles).

## ר&quot;ח ניסן

_Section: ר&quot;ח ניסן_

**Hebrew:**

[mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="195"] (אותו יום נטל עשר עטרות). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="08"]-[mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="207"] (ר"ה למלכים ולרגלים). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="216"] (חל ביום בשבוע שבו חל א' דחה"פ. מאז לכו"ע שואלין ודורשין בהל' הפסח).

**English:**

## Rosh Chodesh Nissan

 (That day took ten crowns). - (The New Year for kings and for the festivals). (It falls on the same day of the week as the first day of Chol HaMoed Pesach. From that time on, according to all opinions, one inquires and expounds upon the laws of Pesach).

---


## ב' ניסן

_Section: ב' ניסן_

**Hebrew:**

[mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="23"].

**English:**

## 2 Nissan



The passing of the Rebbe, the Nasi of our generation, on the second of Nissan, requires us to reflect on the significance of this day in the context of the *avodah* of a *chossid* and the *hiskashrus* to the Rebbe.

The *Gemara* states that "the righteous are called alive even after their death." This is not merely a statement of spiritual survival, but a fundamental principle regarding the nature of the *tzaddik’s* influence. The *avodah* of the *tzaddik*—his *bitul*, his *mesiras nefesh*, and his *dveikus* to Hashem—does not cease when he departs from this world. On the contrary, his influence becomes even more internalized and accessible to those who are connected to him.

When we speak of the *hiskashrus* to the Rebbe, we are not referring to a historical connection to a figure of the past. Rather, we are speaking of a living, ongoing engagement with the *sichos*, the *maamarim*, and the directives that the Rebbe left for us. This is the *avodah* of the *dor hashvi'i*—to take the light of *Chassidus* and bring it into the world, transforming the world into a *dirah b'tachtonim*.

The second of Nissan serves as a *hisorerus* for every *chossid*. It is a time to pause and evaluate: to what extent is my *avodah* aligned with the path the Rebbe paved? How am I internalizing the *kavanah* of the *mitzvos* and the *taanug* of *dveikus* that the Rebbe demanded of us?

Ultimately, the goal is *is'hapcha*—to transform the darkness of *galus* into the light of *Geulah*. This is achieved through the *avodah* of *tashuvah* and *tefillah*, performed with the strength and the *koach* that the Rebbe continues to provide from above. May we merit to see the fulfillment of the Rebbe’s promise, "Immediately to *teshuvah*, immediately to *Geulah*," with the coming of Moshiach now.

---


## י&quot;א ניסן

_Section: י&quot;א ניסן_

**Hebrew:**

88­186. [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="192"]. 95­194. [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="203"]. [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="211"]. [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="221"].

**English:**

## 11 Nissan

---


## י&quot;ג ניסן

_Section: י&quot;ג ניסן_

**Hebrew:**

[mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="23"].

**English:**

## 13 Nissan

 The concept of the 13th of Nissan is that it is the day before the eve of Pesach, and it is the time for *bedikas chametz* (the search for *chametz*).

The inner meaning of this is that *chametz* represents *yeshus* (self-centeredness) and *gaavah* (pride), which are the antithesis of *matzah*—the symbol of *bitul* (self-nullification). The *avodah* of *bedikas chametz* is to search for and remove this *yeshus* from all the hidden corners of one’s heart, even in those places that one does not usually notice or pay attention to.

This search must be conducted with a *ner* (candle), which represents the *neshamah*, as it is written, "The candle of Hashem is the soul of man." Through the light of the *neshamah*, one illuminates the dark corners of the *nefesh habehamis* to identify and nullify any remaining *chametz*.

Ultimately, the goal of this *avodah* is to reach a state of complete *bitul*, which is the preparation required to receive the revelation of the *Ohr Ein Sof* that is drawn down during the days of Pesach. By clearing away the *chametz* of the ego, one creates the necessary vessel to experience the true freedom of *Yetziyas Mitzrayim* in a way that is internalized and lasting.

---

**English:**

## Sukkos

 Regarding the parameters of the mitzvah of Sukkah and whether its obligation applies throughout the entire year. The joy of Sukkos as the source for the joy of mitzvah performance throughout the entire year. Regarding the requirement of kavanah in the mitzvah of Sukkah: is it a remembrance of the Exodus from Egypt, or a remembrance of the miracle of the Clouds of Glory? The holiness of the Sukkah. Regarding the definitions of a Sukkah: made for shade, a temporary dwelling. Regarding the law of "its shade is greater than its sun." Regarding the Haftorah of Shabbos Chol Hamoed Sukkos concerning the day of the arrival of Gog.

[mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="137"] (שתי הלחם — נגלה דתורה ופנימיה"ת, חיבורם יחד). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="270"] (לימודה בבחי' ראי' לעת"ל. שייכותה לשבת).

## Pnimiyus HaTorah

[ftnref_ls32_14] (Nigleh and Nistar correspond to one another like body and soul, and they complete each other). [ftnref_ls32_135] (In the teachings of Chassidus, one finds explanations even according to opinions that were rejected in Halacha). [ftnref_ls32_137] (Shtei HaLechem — the Nigleh of Torah and Pnimiyus HaTorah, their unification). [ftnref_ls32_270] (Its study in the dimension of "seeing" in the time to come. Its connection to Shabbos).

## פסח

_Section: פסח_

**Hebrew:**

[mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="26"] (מבצע פסח, הכנת צרכי החג לכ"א בגשמיות וברוחניות. הגש"פ מצויירת לכל ילד). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="43"] (דילוג וקפיצה שלא בערך). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="44"] (בדין טיבול מצה בחרוסת. ובדיני וטעמי חרוסת). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="48"]-[mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="47"] (במצות מרור). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="48"] (במצות סיפור יצי"מ). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="51"] (בטעם שב­חרוסת אי"צ שיעור כזית משא"כ במרור. בטעם איסור חמץ בכל שהוא. חרוסת זכר לטיט וזכר לתפוח). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="132"] (מצות זכירת יצי"מ שבכל יום נמשכת מזכירת יצי"מ שבליל פסח). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="136"] (פסח ושבועות, אתכפיא ואתהפכא). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="89"]-[mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="187"] (מעות חטים. כל דכפין ייתי ויכול). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="189"] (שייכות מיוחדת לתינוקות). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="196"] (ג' המצות כלים וששת המינים אורות. בהגדה: ט"ו דיינו. בנוסח: אילו הכניסנו כו'. אם קיים תשבי­תו באוכל חמץ בפסח). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="197"] (מיץ ענבים לד' כוסות). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="198"] (חפזון דפסח. דילוג). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="199"] (פסח ע"ש שפסח כו'). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="204"] (חפזון וזריזות בהכנות לפסח). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="205"] (בדיקת החמץ לאור הנר, בעבודה הרוחנית). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="208"] (לידת ישראל כעם). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="211"] (השם: חג ה­מצות). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="212"] (פסח ושבת. קביעותו בחודש האביב, בט"ו בחודש). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="216"] (הראשון וה­יסוד של ג' הרגלים). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="217"] (בתקופת ה­אביב). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="225"] (המברקים לחה"פ).
ע"ע [mafteach\_goterm file="ls32" goterm="מצה"]. [mafteach\_goterm file="ls32" goterm="מצרים"]. [mafteach\_goterm file="ls32" goterm="עומר"]. [mafteach\_goterm file="ls32" displayterm="ק"פ" goterm="קרבן פסח"]. [mafteach\_goterm file="ls32" goterm="רשות"]. [mafteach\_goterm file="ls32" goterm="שבת הגדול"].

**English:**

## Pesach

[mafteach_gopage file="ls32" gopage="26"] (Pesach campaign; preparing the needs of the Yom Tov for everyone, both physically and spiritually. An illustrated Haggadah for every child). [mafteach_gopage file="ls32" gopage="43"] (Skipping and leaping, transcending all proportion). [mafteach_gopage file="ls32" gopage="44"] (The law of dipping matzah in charoses; the laws and reasons for charoses). [mafteach_gopage file="ls32" gopage="48"]-[mafteach_gopage file="ls32" gopage="47"] (The mitzvah of maror). [mafteach_gopage file="ls32" gopage="48"] (The mitzvah of recounting the Exodus from Mitzrayim). [mafteach_gopage file="ls32" gopage="51"] (The reason why charoses does not require a kezayis measurement, unlike maror. The reason for the prohibition of chametz in any amount. Charoses as a remembrance of the clay and a remembrance of the apple). [mafteach_gopage file="ls32" gopage="132"] (The mitzvah of remembering the Exodus from Mitzrayim every day is drawn from the remembrance of the Exodus on the night of Pesach). [mafteach_gopage file="ls32" gopage="136"] (Pesach and Shavuos; iskafya and is'hapcha). [mafteach_gopage file="ls32" gopage="89"]-[mafteach_gopage file="ls32" gopage="187"] (Maos Chittim; "Let all who are hungry come and eat"). [mafteach_gopage file="ls32" gopage="189"] (A special connection to children). [mafteach_gopage file="ls32" gopage="196"] (The three matzos as vessels and the six species as lights. In the Haggadah: the fifteen steps of Dayeinu. In the text: "Had He brought us in," etc. If one fulfills "you shall remove" by eating chametz on Pesach). [mafteach_gopage file="ls32" gopage="197"] (Grape juice for the Arba Kosos). [mafteach_gopage file="ls32" gopage="198"] (The haste of Pesach; skipping). [mafteach_gopage file="ls32" gopage="199"] (Pesach, because He "passed over," etc.). [mafteach_gopage file="ls32" gopage="204"] (Haste and alacrity in preparations for Pesach). [mafteach_gopage file="ls32" gopage="205"] (Bedikas Chametz by the light of a candle, in spiritual avodah). [mafteach_gopage file="ls32" gopage="208"] (The birth of Yisroel as a nation). [mafteach_gopage file="ls32" gopage="211"] (The name: Chag HaMatzos). [mafteach_gopage file="ls32" gopage="212"] (Pesach and Shabbos. Its fixed time in the month of spring, on the fifteenth of the month). [mafteach_gopage file="ls32" gopage="216"] (The first and foundation of the three Regalim). [mafteach_gopage file="ls32" gopage="217"] (In the spring season). [mafteach_gopage file="ls32" gopage="225"] (The telegrams for the holiday of Pesach).

See also [mafteach_goterm file="ls32" goterm="מצה"], [mafteach_goterm file="ls32" goterm="מצרים"], [mafteach_goterm file="ls32" goterm="עומר"], [mafteach_goterm file="ls32" displayterm="ק"פ" goterm="קרבן פסח"], [mafteach_goterm file="ls32" goterm="רשות"], [mafteach_goterm file="ls32" goterm="שבת הגדול"].

## פקוח נפש

_Section: פקוח נפש_

**Hebrew:**

[mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="120"] (בדין לא תעמוד על דם רעך). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="122"] (אם יש חיוב להכנס בספק סכנה כדי להציל חבירו ממיתה). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="125"] (אם חייבים להציל המאבד עצמו לדעת או המעמיד עצמו בסכנה. אם גם הצלת הנפש משמד וכיו"ב הו"ע פקו"נ).

**English:**

## Pikuach Nefesh

 Regarding the law of "Do not stand idly by the blood of your neighbor." Whether there is an obligation to enter into a situation of potential danger in order to save one's fellow from death. Whether one is obligated to save someone who is attempting suicide or placing themselves in danger. Whether saving a soul from spiritual destruction (shemad) and the like is also considered a matter of Pikuach Nefesh.

## צדקה

_Section: צדקה_

**Hebrew:**

[mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="155"] (אמירה לעני אי הוי בגדר נדר). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="201"] (בצדקה וגמ"ח הוא שלוחו של הקב"ה ליתן להעני). **English:**

## Tzedakah

[mafteach_gopage file="ls32" gopage="155"] (A verbal promise to a poor person does not constitute a vow). [mafteach_gopage file="ls32" gopage="201"] (In tzedakah and gemilas chasadim, one acts as an agent of Hashem to provide for the poor). [mafteach_gopage file="ls32" gopage="219"] (More than what the householder does for the poor, etc.). [mafteach_gopage file="ls32" gopage="254"] (Increasing in tzedakah).
See also [mafteach_goterm file="ls32" displayterm="Gemilas Chasadim" goterm="גמילות חסדים"]. [mafteach_goterm file="ls32" displayterm="Neshek" goterm="נרות שבת קודש"]. [mafteach_goterm file="ls32" goterm="קטנים"]. [mafteach_goterm file="ls32" displayterm="Torah, Avodah, and Gemilas Chasadim" goterm="תורה עבודה וגמילות חסדים"].

[mafteach_gopage file="ls32" gopage="137"] (The foundation of avodah). [mafteach_gopage file="ls32" gopage="179"] (Even mitzvos that are understood by the intellect must be performed with kabbalas ol). [mafteach_gopage file="ls32" gopage="180"] (Kabbalas ol experienced with vitality and taanug).

**English:**

## Kedosh, Kedushah

(Kedushah does not move from its place). (The back-and-forth discussion regarding various matters of Kedushah: whether a more stringent Kedushah also contains within it a more lenient Kedushah). (The Kedushah of the soul causes one to become like the Shechinah. Kedushah and Taharah).

ע"ע [mafteach\_goterm file="ls32" goterm="בגדי כהונה"].

**English:**

## Kodesh HaKodashim

 (Moshe’s entry to hear the Divine utterance). (The entry of the Kohen Gadol into the Holy of Holies, in the service of Yom Kippur). (The ultimate intent of the Kohen Gadol’s service in the Kodesh HaKodashim is not to withdraw from the world, but rather, on the contrary, to draw its holiness down into the world).
See also [ftnref_32_goterm_bigdei_kehunah] (Priestly garments).

**English:**

## Korbanos

 (A pleasing aroma). (The rationale of the mitzvah. Korbanos prior to Matan Torah). (Their fulfillment as a royal decree and bitul without any conceptualization or calculation. "My bread for My fire"). (Olah and Chatas, their distinctions). (On the rationale of the Chatas offering). (On the distinctions between Chatas and Asham). (As above. Their essence is primarily the thought and kavanah of the person. From the root word for "drawing near" [kiruv]). (From the male and from the female. Distinctions of species and types in Korbanos. The rationale of the mitzvah. Kiruv). (Definitions in the law: "We will render the offering of our lips," and "Whoever occupies himself with the Torah of the Olah is as if he offered it," etc.). (On the laws of receiving and transporting the blood). (After the Churban). (Korbanos and Ketores — their distinction. From the root word for "drawing near" [kiruv]). (Kavanah is primary in Korbanos). (They are among the Chukim). (As above).
See also [ftnref_goterm_1] (Mizbeach).

## שבועות, חג השבועות

_Section: שבועות, חג השבועות_

**Hebrew:**

[mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="134"] (בגדר היתר מנחה חדשה ע"י שתי הלחם). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="136"] (שתי הלחם מאכל אדם, עומר מאכל בהמה. שתי הלחם: חמץ, את­הפכא). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="137"] (שתי הלחם, התחברות הלחם עם הכבשים — בעבודה הרוחנית. שתי הלחם: תושב"כ ותושבע"פ, נסתר דתורה ונגלה דתורה). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="139"] (שתי הלחם, מנחה חדשה). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="228"] (קביעות חה"ש למי שעבר את קו התאריך באמצע ימי הספירה).

**English:**

## Shavuos, Chag HaShavuos

 Regarding the parameters of permitting the *minchah chadashah* (new grain offering) through the *shtei halechem* (two loaves). The *shtei halechem* is human food, whereas the *omer* is animal food. The *shtei halechem* is *chametz*, representing *is'hapcha*. The *shtei halechem* represents the unification of the bread with the sheep in spiritual *avodah*. The *shtei halechem* represents *Torah Shebichtav* and *Torah Shebe'al Peh*, the *nistar* of Torah and the *nigleh* of Torah. The *shtei halechem* as a *minchah chadashah*. Determining the date of *Chag HaShavuos* for one who crossed the International Date Line during the days of *Sefirah*.

**English:**

## Shabbos

[mafteach_gopage file="ls32" gopage="64"] (A holy convocation. Whether one is obligated to purify oneself on Shabbos, similar to Yom Tov). [mafteach_gopage file="ls32" gopage="65"] (It is sanctified and established). [mafteach_gopage file="ls32" gopage="130"] (Regarding the statement of our Sages that Yosef observed the Shabbos before it was given). [mafteach_gopage file="ls32" gopage="131"] (Shabbos and Yom Tov are among the mitzvos that fall under the category of testimony. It is a sign for all time). [mafteach_gopage file="ls32" gopage="132"] (The mitzvah of remembering the Shabbos). [mafteach_gopage file="ls32" gopage="198"] (Before Shabbos). [mafteach_gopage file="ls32" gopage="212"] (Its core point is the cessation of work. "A Shabbos to Hashem your G-d" — dedication to matters of holiness. A remembrance of the Exodus from Egypt). [mafteach_gopage file="ls32" gopage="216"] (Shabbos and Yom Tov are a remembrance of the Exodus from Egypt). [mafteach_gopage file="ls32" gopage="254"] (The three days following Shabbos). [mafteach_gopage file="ls32" gopage="257"] (The prohibition against Jewish ships setting sail on Shabbos). [mafteach_gopage file="ls32" gopage="259"] (The greatness of saving a Jewish soul from desecrating the Shabbos). [mafteach_gopage file="ls32" gopage="266"] (The virtue of a farbrengen on Shabbos. Even an unlearned person feels the awe of Shabbos, etc.).
See also [mafteach_goterm file="ls32" displayterm="Yom Tov" goterm="יום טוב"]. [mafteach_goterm file="ls32" goterm="נרות שבת קודש"].

## שבת הגדול

_Section: שבת הגדול_

## Shabbos HaGadol

---


## שבת מברכים

_Section: שבת מברכים_

**Hebrew:**

[mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="265"].

**English:**

## Shabbos Mevarchim

 The concept of Shabbos Mevarchim — blessing the new month — is that it serves as the source of blessing for the entire month. As is well known, the month of the year corresponds to the soul’s service in the world, and the blessing of the month provides the strength and vitality for this service throughout the coming days.

This is particularly relevant when we consider the connection between the specific month and the avodah of the Chassid. Through the act of blessing the month on Shabbos, we draw down the energy of the *Ein Sof* into the *Seder Hishtalshelus*, ensuring that the *avodah* of the month is performed with *bitul* and *kavanah*. 

Seemingly, one might ask: if the blessing is drawn down on Shabbos, why is it necessary to engage in *avodah* throughout the weekdays? But in truth, the blessing of Shabbos is merely the potential; the *avodah* of the weekdays is the actualization. This is the mechanism of *ratzo v'shov* — the soul ascends to *dveikus* on Shabbos, and then descends into the mundane reality of the week to transform it, thereby revealing the *Elokus* that was latent within the blessing. 

And with this we can understand the importance of *hiskashrus* to the Rebbe during this time. By connecting to the Nasi of the generation, one gains the clarity to channel the blessing of the month into practical *mitzvos* and *avodah*, ensuring that the entire month is infused with the light of the *maamarim* and *sichos* that guide our path.

---

## שלמה, רש&quot;י

_Section: שלמה, רש&quot;י_

**Hebrew:**

[mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="59"]-[mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="58"] (בפי' עה"ת דרכו לפרש ולא להעתיק לשונות רז"ל כצורתן). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="86"] (פי' אינו ספר הלכות). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="93"] (כשאינו מפרש דבריו הוא מפני שסומך על המבואר בה­כתובים לפני"ז). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="121"] (אף אם דרך המקרא להשתמש בוא"ו במקום "או" צ"ע אם גם רש"י יכתוב כן שהרי כתב פירושו בלשון צחה וברורה). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="123"] (מביא דוגמאות בפי' מן ההוה ורגיל).

**English:**

## Shlomo, Rashi

 In his commentary on the Torah, it is his practice to explain rather than to copy the language of our Sages verbatim.

 His commentary is not a book of halacha.

 When he does not explain his words, it is because he relies on what has already been clarified in the preceding verses.

 Even if it is the way of Scripture to use the letter *vav* in place of "or," it is a matter requiring study whether Rashi would also write in this manner, given that he wrote his commentary in clear and precise language.

 He brings examples in his commentary from the current and the commonplace.

## תורה עבודה וגמילות חסדים

_Section: תורה עבודה וגמילות חסדים_

**Hebrew:**

[mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="254"].

**English:**

## Torah, Avodah, and Gemilas Chasadim



The Mishnah in Pirkei Avos states: "The world stands on three things: on Torah, on Avodah, and on Gemilas Chasadim." 

Seemingly, we must understand the order of these three pillars. Why is Torah mentioned first, followed by Avodah, and finally Gemilas Chasadim? Furthermore, what is the internal connection between these three, and how do they collectively sustain the world?

To understand this, we must first clarify the nature of each of these three pillars.

Torah represents the wisdom of Hashem, which is entirely transcendent. Through the study of Torah, a person connects their intellect to the Divine intellect. This is an act of "reception"—the person draws down the light of Elokus into their own mind.

Avodah, by contrast, represents the service of the heart—specifically, tefillah. Unlike Torah, which is the descent of Divine wisdom to man, Avodah is the ascent of the soul from below to above. It is the process of refining one's own character and emotions, bringing them into a state of bitul before Hashem.

Gemilas Chasadim represents the manifestation of this holiness in the world of action. It is not merely a private act of piety, but the extension of one's own goodness to another. It is the bridge between the internal state of the individual and the reality of the world around them.

Thus, the order is precise: First, one must engage in Torah to acquire the proper perspective and Divine wisdom. Second, one must engage in Avodah to internalize that wisdom and transform one's own heart. And third, one must engage in Gemilas Chasadim to project that transformation outward, thereby sustaining the world.

---

## תרמוד

_Section: תרמוד_

**Hebrew:**

[mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="162"].

**English:**

## Tarmod

 The Gemara states, "The people of Tarmod are not to be accepted [for conversion]." The commentators discuss the reason for this: some say it is because they were involved in the destruction of the Beis Hamikdash, while others say it is because they were involved in the burning of the Beis Hamikdash. 

On the face of it, we must understand: why is this specific group singled out? Throughout the generations, many nations and individuals participated in the destruction of the Beis Hamikdash. Why is it that specifically the people of Tarmod are prohibited from entering the congregation of Hashem, even if they were to seek conversion?

Furthermore, the Gemara concludes that the reason they are not accepted is because they are "mamzerim" (illegitimate). This requires explanation: how does their involvement in the destruction of the Beis Hamikdash render them mamzerim?

But in truth, we can understand this by first examining the spiritual root of Tarmod. The name "Tarmod" is associated with the concept of *meridah* (rebellion). Their essence is one of opposition to the holiness of the Beis Hamikdash, which is the point of connection between Hashem and the Jewish people. Because they represent the absolute antithesis of this connection, their very existence is a negation of the sanctity of the Jewish people. Therefore, they are fundamentally incompatible with the holiness of the Jewish nation, to the extent that they are categorized as mamzerim—those who are excluded from the congregation of Hashem.

---

_Section: תצוה_

**Hebrew:**

## Shemini

(Sometimes it is read eight times).
9:8-9 And Aharon approached... and the sons of Aharon brought...
9:9 And he dipped his finger in the blood...
9:12 And he slaughtered the burnt offering, and they presented it to him... (and Rashi: an expression of extending and offering).
And he threw it upon the altar:
9:13 And they presented the burnt offering to him...
9:14 And he burned the fat upon the burnt offering:
9:18 And the sons of Aharon presented it to him...
9:23 In Rashi, s.v. "And he came": Why did Moshe enter with Aharon... and the Shechinah descended...
In Rashi, s.v. "And they went out": All seven days of the inauguration... he said to him... Aharon my brother...
9:23-24 And the glory of Hashem appeared... and fire went out from before Hashem...
9:24 And all the people saw and sang (and Rashi):
10:1 And they offered strange fire before Hashem...
10:2 And fire went out from before Hashem...
10:3 This is what Hashem spoke... "By those who are near to Me I will be sanctified" (and Rashi: I knew that it would be sanctified...):
10:10-11 To distinguish... to teach... by the hand of Moshe:
11:8 And you shall not touch their carcass (and Rashi: Could it be that Yisroel are warned...):
11:43 Do not make yourselves detestable...
11:44 And you shall sanctify yourselves and you shall be holy...

## Tazria

Rashi at the beginning of the parsha regarding the subject of nega'im: [ftnref_ls32_89].
13:2 "When a person shall have in the skin of his flesh...": [ftnref_ls32_77]. [ftnref_ls32_89].
One of his sons, the Kohanim: [ftnref_ls32_87].
In Rashi: It is a gezeiras hakasuv, etc.: [ftnref_ls32_87].
13:3 In Rashi: White hair is a sign of tumah; it is a gezeiras hakasuv: [ftnref_ls32_79].
13:6 In Rashi: It has dimmed from its appearance, etc.: [ftnref_ls32_86].
13:9 "When a plague of tzaraas shall be in a person...": [ftnref_ls32_77]. [ftnref_ls32_81].
13:10 In Rashi: Even though michyah was not mentioned, etc.: [ftnref_ls32_77].
13:11 In Rashi: It is an old plague, etc.: [ftnref_ls32_80].
13:12 "And if the tzaraas breaks out...": [ftnref_ls32_79].
"To all the sights of the eyes of the Kohen": [ftnref_ls32_84].
In Rashi: Excluding a Kohen whose vision has dimmed: [ftnref_ls32_85].
13:13 "He is tahor": [ftnref_ls32_79].
13:14 "And on the day that raw flesh appears in it, he shall be tamei" (and Rashi: If it grew, etc.): [ftnref_ls32_80]. [ftnref_ls32_81].
In Rashi: And likewise for the foot, etc.: [ftnref_ls32_60].
13:24 In Rashi: And its signs are the same as a burn and a boil, etc.: [ftnref_ls32_79].
13:40 "He is a bald man; he is tahor": [ftnref_ls32_80].

## Emor

21:1 Speak to the Kohanim, etc. 
21:2 Speak, etc., and you shall say (and Rashi: to admonish the adults regarding the minors): 
21:6 [Korbanos are called bread. And similarly in the verses below]: 
21:22 And he may eat of the holy things (and Rashi: that Moshe ate the meat of the Miluim, etc.): 
22:10 In Rashi: a hired worker... who goes out after six [years], etc.: 
22:11 In Rashi: a Canaanite slave who is acquired for his body (to eat of the Terumah): 
22:24 In your land you shall not do, etc. (and Rashi: that it is impossible to say, etc.): 
23:2 The appointed times of Hashem, which you shall proclaim as holy convocations: 
23:3 A holy convocation: 
[In the order of the Mo'adim — after Shabbos: Pesach, Omer, etc.]: 
23:10 The beginning of your harvest, etc.: 
23:14 And bread, and parched grain, and fresh ears you shall not eat, etc.: 
23:15 Seven complete weeks shall there be: 
23:16 A new Mincha: 
23:17 They shall be baked leavened: 
23:24 A holy convocation: 
23:35 In Rashi: Sanctify it with clean clothing, etc.: 
23:42-43 You shall dwell in Sukkos, etc., for I made [the Children of Yisrael] dwell in Sukkos, etc.:

## Sanhedrin

2a: Dedications [are valid] with three [judges], etc. 
34b: [The Gemara] equates [the laws of] disputes to [the laws of] nega'im. 
In Meiri regarding a Kohen whose eyesight has dimmed, [asking] if he may rule on nega'im. 
73a: Regarding "Do not stand idly by the blood of your neighbor." 
83b: At the time that their garments are upon them, their Kehunah is [upon them], etc. 
97a: The son of Dovid will not come until the entire kingdom turns to heresy, etc. 
97b: Regarding the war of Gog and Magog and the other events of the days of Moshiach (and Rashi: that the subjugation ends and Moshiach comes): 184-5.
Rabbi Yehudah said to him... "And you shall not be redeemed with money," etc. 
98a: The son of Dovid will only come in a generation that is entirely meritorious or entirely guilty. 
101a: "If you will listen, I will not place [upon you any of the diseases]," etc. (and Chiddushei Aggados).

And regarding the Gibeonites, etc., because they were brazen, etc. [mafteach_gopage file="ls32" gopage="201"].

פ"ד ה"א סדר כל המעשים שביום זה כו': [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="108"].
ספ"ד ויוצא לביתו וכל העם מלוין אותו עד ביתו: 09­108.
ויו"ט הי' עושה על שיצא כו': [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="106"].

## Laws of the Avodah of Yom Kippur

And he would make a Yom Tov because he emerged, etc. [ftnref_ls32_106]

## הלכות מעילה

_Section: הלכות מעילה_

**Hebrew:**

בסופן והחוקים הן המצות שאין טעמן ידוע כו': [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="4"]. [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="174"].
כל הקרבנות כולן מכלל החוקים הן: [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="15"].
[מהחילוקים בין סיום ס' עבודה לס' קרבנות]: [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="15"].

**English:**

## Laws of Me'ilah

At their conclusion, the *chukim* are the *mitzvos* whose reasons are not known, etc. [mafteach_gopage file="ls32" gopage="4"]. [mafteach_gopage file="ls32" gopage="174"].

All *korbanos* are included in the category of *chukim*. [mafteach_gopage file="ls32" gopage="15"].

[Regarding the distinctions between the conclusion of *Sefer Avodah* and *Sefer Korbanos*]: [mafteach_gopage file="ls32" gopage="15"].

## Laws of the Korban Pesach

At the end of Chapter 1: One who slaughters the Pesach offering and it is found to be a *ba'al mum* [blemished animal], etc..

## הלכות שגגות

_Section: הלכות שגגות_

**Hebrew:**

פ"ג ה"ג המפריש חטאתו על החלב . . לא יביאנה כו', הפריש חטאתו על חלב שאכל אמש כו': [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="7"].
פ"ג ה"י השבים המאמינים בכפרתן: [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="8"].
פ"ו ה"ט [אכל שני זיתי חלב כו' מביא שנים]: [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="7"].

**English:**

## Hilchos Shegagos

Chapter 6, Halacha 3: One who designates his chatas for a cheilev [that he ate]... shall not bring it, etc.; [or if] he designated his chatas for a cheilev that he ate yesterday, etc.:.

Chapter 6, Halacha 10: Those who repent and believe in their atonement:.

Chapter 6, Halacha 5: [If he ate two kezaysim of cheilev, etc., he brings two]:.

## הלכות תמורה

_Section: הלכות תמורה_

**Hebrew:**

בסופן אע"פ שכל חוקי כו' ראוי להתבונן בהם כו': [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="15"]. [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="174"].
[חוקים אין בהם טעם נגלה]: [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="4"].
יראה לי שזש"ה והי' הוא ותמורתו כו' ירדה תורה לסוף מחשבת האדם כו': [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="176"].
רוב דיני התורה אינן אלא עצות כו' וליישר כל המעשים: [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="175"].
[סיום ס' קרבנות בהל' תמורה]: [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="174"].

**English:**

## Laws of Temurah

At the end of the laws, [it is stated]: "Even though all the *chukim*..." [it is] appropriate to contemplate them.....

[Chukim have no revealed reason]:.

It appears to me that this is the meaning of the verse, "Then it and its exchange shall be..." — the Torah descends to the depth of human thought....

Most of the laws of the Torah are nothing but counsel... and to straighten all deeds:.

[Conclusion of Sefer Korbanos in Hilchos Temurah]:.

---

## הלכות טומאת צרעת

_Section: הלכות טומאת צרעת_

**Hebrew:**

פ"א ה"א בכ"מ: [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="78"].
פ"א ה"ג הכל כמראה אחד הוא חשוב: [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="78"].
פ"ז ה"א הוחלט . . ואח"כ פרחה הצרעת . . טהור אבל . . ונפטר . . טמא: [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="80"].
פ"ז ה"ג [ע"פ וביום הראות בו בשר חי]: [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="81"].
פ"ז ה"ד אף ראשי איברים כו' מטמאין ומעכבין את הנהפך כו': [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="81"].
פ"ח הי"ב פשה הנתק על כל ראשו כו': [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="80"].
פ"ט ה"א­ב הכל כשרין לראות את הנגעים . . הטומאה והטהרה תלוי' בכהן: [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="87"].
פ"ט ה"ה חלל פסול לראיית נגעים כו': [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="87"].
סומא כו' לא יראה את הנגעים כו': [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="84"].
פ"ט הי"ב אין הכהן זקוק כו' לכל מראה עיני הכהן: [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="84"].
פי"א ה"ד תגלחת המצורע וטבילתו: [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="233"].
פי"ב ה"ח אחר שפטרו בא כולו ירקרק . . טהור: [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="80"].
בסופן שיחת הרשעים כו' שיחת כשרי ישראל כו': [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="97"].

**English:**

## Laws of Tzaaras Impurity

Chapter 1, Law 1: In every place [ftnref_ls32_78].
Chapter 1, Law 3: Everything is considered as one appearance [ftnref_ls32_78].
Chapter 7, Law 1: It was declared absolute... and afterwards the tzaaras spread... he is tahor, but... and he is exempt... he is tamei [ftnref_ls32_80].
Chapter 7, Law 3: [Based on "And on the day that raw flesh appears in it"] [ftnref_ls32_81].
Chapter 7, Law 4: Even the tips of the limbs, etc., render tamei and delay the transformation, etc. [ftnref_ls32_81].
Chapter 8, Law 12: If the netek (scall) spread over his entire head, etc. [ftnref_ls32_80].
Chapter 9, Laws 1-2: Everyone is qualified to inspect the negaim... the tumah and taharah depend on the Kohen [ftnref_ls32_87].
Chapter 9, Law 5: A challal (a Kohen born of an invalid union) is disqualified from inspecting negaim, etc. [ftnref_ls32_87].
A blind person, etc., shall not inspect the negaim, etc. [ftnref_ls32_84].
Chapter 9, Law 12: The Kohen is not required, etc., to [inspect] according to all that the eyes of the Kohen see [ftnref_ls32_84].
Chapter 11, Law 4: The shaving of the metzora and his immersion [ftnref_ls32_233].
Chapter 12, Law 8: After he declared him exempt, it turned entirely greenish-yellow... he is tahor [ftnref_ls32_80].
At their conclusion: The conversation of the wicked, etc., the conversation of the kosher ones of Yisrael, etc. [ftnref_ls32_97].

**English:**

## Laws of Robbery and Lost Property

Chapter 1, Law 5: For the verse has transformed it into a positive commandment, etc. [ftnref_ls32_117]
Chapter 1, Law 3: One who takes another person’s property by force. [ftnref_ls32_115]
Chapter 1, Law 5: One who steals a beam, etc.; the ordinance for the penitent, etc. [ftnref_ls32_116]
Chapter 1, Law 11: Desire leads to coveting, etc.; robbery, etc. [ftnref_ls32_114]
End of Chapter 1: Anyone who steals, etc., takes his soul, etc. [ftnref_ls32_112]
It is an ordinance of the Sages that we do not accept [restitution] from him, etc. [ftnref_ls32_116]
Chapter 7, Law 7: That there is no monetary value less than a perutah. [ftnref_ls32_115]

## Laws of Kings

[The name of these laws]: [mafteach_gopage file="ls32" gopage="277"].

## ספר המצות

_Section: ספר המצות_

**Hebrew:**

## Sefer HaMitzvos

The root of the fourth [mitzvah], "And you shall sanctify yourselves, etc.," is among the general commandments: [mafteach_gopage file="ls32" gopage="68"].
Positive Commandment 245, its laws and details: [mafteach_gopage file="ls32" gopage="160"].
Negative Commandment 347, "And you shall not lie carnally with your neighbor's wife, etc.," is a prohibition for the man alone: [mafteach_gopage file="ls32" gopage="245"].

## פירוש המשניות

_Section: פירוש המשניות_

**Hebrew:**

בהקדמה הלך רבינו הקדוש אחר פרשת המועדות דאמור: [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="127"].
**שביעית**
בסופו בפי' המטלטלין נקנין במשיכה וכל המקיים את דברו כו': [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="154"].
**פסחים**
פ"י מ"ג חרוסת זכר לטיט: [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="46"].
**יומא**
פ"ז מ"ד בהיו"ט שהי' עושה הכה"ג כו': [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="107"].
**נזיר**
פ"ח מ"ו דתגלחת מצורע אחר הטבילה: [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="233"].

**English:**

## Commentary on the Mishnayos

In the introduction, Rabbeinu HaKadosh follows the order of the festivals as presented in the parsha of the *moadim* in *Emor*: [ftnref_ls32_127].

**Sheviis**
At the end, in the explanation that movable property is acquired through *meshichah*, and whoever keeps his word, etc.: [ftnref_ls32_154].

**Pesachim**
Chapter 10, Mishnah 3: *Charoses* is a remembrance of the clay: [ftnref_ls32_46].

**Yoma**
Chapter 7, Mishnah 4: Regarding the Yom Tov that the Kohen Gadol would observe, etc.: [ftnref_ls32_107].

**Nazir**
Chapter 8, Mishnah 6: Regarding the shaving of the *metzora* after the immersion: [ftnref_ls32_233].

---


## שמונה פרקים

_Section: שמונה פרקים_

**Hebrew:**

רב, ב אם אני חייב קרבן תודה כו': [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="107"].
ריא, ב קרבן ענינו קירוב: [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="16"].

## Shelah

13b: The Torah speaks of the supernal realms while hinting at the lower realms: [mafteach_gopage file="ls32" gopage="74"].
140b: The reading of the *Parsha* of the *Nesi'im* during the month of Nissan: [mafteach_gopage file="ls32" gopage="19"].
142a: Regarding the phrasing of the *Seder* of *Korban Pesach*: [mafteach_gopage file="ls32" gopage="36"] and onwards.
162a: On the interpretation of "The Egyptians treated us harshly" (*Vayare'u osanu hamitzrim*): [mafteach_gopage file="ls32" gopage="50"].
162b: The connection between "I am He and no other" and "I am Hashem your healer": [mafteach_gopage file="ls32" gopage="74"].
202b: If I am obligated to bring a *korban todah*, etc.: [mafteach_gopage file="ls32" gopage="107"].
211b: The concept of a *korban* is *kiruv* (drawing near): [mafteach_gopage file="ls32" gopage="16"].

## תניא

_Section: תניא_

**Hebrew:**

###### לקוטי אמרים

פ"ז ע"י אכו"ש יורד כו': [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="138"].
פי"ג ואדרבה נתחזק יותר כו' באכו"ש כו': [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="138"].
פכ"ג האבות הן הן המרכבה כו': [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="25"].
פכ"ה ויחוד זה למעלה הוא נצחי לעולם ועד: [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="133"].
פל"א יצי"מ . . ברח . . שהרע . . בתקפו כו': [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="136"].
פל"ב נק' כל ישראל אחים כו' רק שהגופים מחולקים: [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="219"]. [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="245"].
פל"ז הואיל ובמעות אלו הי' יכול לקנות חיי נפשו: [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="113"]. [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="114"]. [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="116"].
פמ"א [קבלת עול ראשית העבודה]: [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="137"].

###### אגרת התשובה

פ"א לכך אין כפרה . . עד יוהכ"פ כו': [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="10"].
דהיינו שיגמור בלבו כו': [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="9"].
פ"ב שולח דורון . . שיתרצה לו . . ולשון מכפרת כו': [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="8"].
רפ"ג חטאת . . על כל פעם ופעם כו': [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="8"].
פ"ג צדקה . . יותר מחומש . . ולא גרעא כו': [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="194"].
פ"ז ואפילו בכפילת חטא אחד פעמים רבות מאד: [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="11"].
בסופו וכמ"ש לעיל וכו' בחי' סוכ"ע: [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="227"].

###### אגרת הקדש

ס"י צדקה . . יותר מחומש . . כל אשר לאיש כו': [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="194"].
סי"ט אי"ז . . תכלית הטעם וגבולו: [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="180"].
סי' זך ונלכה באורחותיו נס"ו: [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="26"].
צדיקא דאתפטר כו': [mafteach\_gopage file="ls32" gopage="23"].

**English:**

###### Likutei Amarim

Chapter 7: Through the *achish* [the acronym for *eish*, *cherev*, *shoresh*] it descends, etc. [mafteach_gopage file="ls32" gopage="138"].
Chapter 13: And on the contrary, one is strengthened even more, etc., through *achish*, etc. [mafteach_gopage file="ls32" gopage="138"].
Chapter 23: The Patriarchs are the *Merkavah*, etc. [mafteach_gopage file="ls32" gopage="25"].
Chapter 25: And this *yichud* above is eternal, forever and ever. [mafteach_gopage file="ls32" gopage="133"].
Chapter 31: The Exodus from Egypt... fleeing... that the evil... in its strength, etc. [mafteach_gopage file="ls32" gopage="136"].
Chapter 32: Called "all Israel are brothers," etc., only that the bodies are divided. [mafteach_gopage file="ls32" gopage="219"]. [mafteach_gopage file="ls32" gopage="245"].
Chapter 37: Since with these funds one could have purchased the life of his soul. [mafteach_gopage file="ls32" gopage="113"]. [mafteach_gopage file="ls32" gopage="114"]. [mafteach_gopage file="ls32" gopage="116"].
Chapter 41: [Acceptance of the yoke is the beginning of *avodah*]. [mafteach_gopage file="ls32" gopage="137"].

###### Iggeret HaTeshuvah

Chapter 1: Therefore there is no atonement... until Yom Kippur, etc. [mafteach_gopage file="ls32" gopage="10"].
That is, that he resolves in his heart, etc. [mafteach_gopage file="ls32" gopage="9"].
Chapter 2: He sends a gift... that He may be appeased... and the tongue atones, etc. [mafteach_gopage file="ls32" gopage="8"].
Beginning of Chapter 3: A sin-offering... for each and every time, etc. [mafteach_gopage file="ls32" gopage="8"].
Chapter 3: *Tzedakah*... more than a *chomesh*... and it is not diminished, etc. [mafteach_gopage file="ls32" gopage="194"].
Chapter 7: And even regarding the repetition of one sin very many times. [mafteach_gopage file="ls32" gopage="11"].
At its end: And as written above, etc., the aspect of *Sovev Kol Almin*. [mafteach_gopage file="ls32" gopage="227"].

###### Iggeret HaKodesh

Epistle 10: *Tzedakah*... more than a *chomesh*... all that a man has, etc. [mafteach_gopage file="ls32" gopage="194"].
Epistle 19: It is not... the ultimate reason and its boundary. [mafteach_gopage file="ls32" gopage="180"].
Epistle 27: And we shall walk in His ways, *netzach selah va'ed*. [mafteach_gopage file="ls32" gopage="26"].
A *tzaddik* who has departed, etc. [mafteach_gopage file="ls32" gopage="23"].
